subject: Sociology
title: Defining Religion Sociologically
description: A fast breakdown of Defining Religion Sociologically for builders.

hook.text: Ask ten people what religion means and you get ten answers. Sociologists skip belief and study behavior.

buildup.text: Durkheim defined religion as a system of beliefs and practices about sacred things that unite a community.

discovery.text: The key isn't what people believe but how shared rituals create social bonds and moral boundaries.

twist.text: By this definition, nationalism and sports fandom share structural features with traditional religions.

climax.text: Religion is a social institution first. Personal faith matters, but sociology studies the collective.

punchline.text: Religion is what groups do together.

quiz.question: What did Durkheim focus on when defining religion?
